coreIdea: In a right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of squares of the other two sides.
No three positive integers satisfy aⁿ + bⁿ = cⁿ for any integer n greater than two.
e^(iπ) + 1 = 0 — the most beautiful equation connecting five fundamental constants.
Integration and differentiation are inverse operations — the area under a curve relates to its antiderivative.
π can be computed by an infinite series: π/4 = 1 - 1/3 + 1/5 - 1/7 + 1/9 ...
Every even integer greater than 2 is the sum of two prime numbers — unproven but verified to enormous limits.
A solid sphere can be decomposed and reassembled into two identical copies of itself — true in theory, impossible in practice.
